rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, JULY 24, 2007, LINE 1. DEFENDANT SEQUOIA VENTURES INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS OFF CALENDAR PER MOVING PARTY. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: VIKAS HOTEL, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. SYED M. Y. ALI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion An D Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Terms Of Stipulated Settlement; Declarations Sam Patel; Richard M. Bryan In Support
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, JULY 24, 2007, LINE 2. PLAINTIFF VIKAS HOTEL, L.L.C.'S Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Terms Of Stipulated Settlement IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS PRESENTED AN APPRAISAL PREPARED BY AN INDEPENDENT THIRD PARTY FOR A LENDER BANK THAT REFLECTS A FAIR MARKET VALUE OF $2.6 MILLION BASED ON COMPARABLE SALES AND $2.525 MILLION BASED ON AN INCOME ANALYSIS. PLAINTIFF CHALLENGES ONLY THE LATTER FIGURE. BASED ON COMPARABLE SALES, THERE IS ADEQUATE SECURITY TO SECURE THE SETTLEMENT AMOUNT OF $312,500 BASED ON AN OBJECTIVE STANDARD APPLICABLE TO COMMERCIAL TRANSACTIONS UNDER MATTEI V HOPPER (1958) 51C 2D. 119. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: INNOVATIVE XPRESSIONS, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. GOOGLE, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, JULY 24, 2007, LINE 7. DEFENDANT GOOGLE INC. AND NEVENGINEERING, INC.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. LEAVE TO AMEND WITHIN TWENTY DAYS OF SERVICE OF THE ORDER IS GRANTED. SEE COURT'S MINUTES FOR JULY 23, 2007 AND AMENDED MINUTES FOR JULY 23, 2007 FOR THE BALANCE OF THE RULING. A COPY OF THE ENTIRE RULING IS BEING FAXED TO RODERICK McLEOD AT JONES DAY AND STEPHEN TAYLOR AT TAYLOR & CO. LAW OFFICES. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: BRENDA J. WORLEY VS. ROBERT TYSKO et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, JULY 24, 2007, LINE 8. DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA STATE AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. UNDER ROYAL GLOBE, ANY VIABLE COMMON LAW CAUSES OF ACTION SHOULD BE MAINTAINED IN A SEPARATE ACTION. THE CHALLENGED CONDUCT ALLEGED IS PART OF THE NORMAL CLAIM PROCESS. WITH RESPECT TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR FRAUD AND NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION, PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PLEAD RELIANCE AND AS TO THE LATTER CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF ALLEGES ONLY FUTURE EVENTS WHICH ARE NOT ACTIONABLE. THE CLAIM OF NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS FAILS AS A MATTER OF LAW. BURGESS V SUPERIOR COURT 2 CAL 4TH 1064 (1992). NO OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT HAS BEEN ALLEGED TO ESTABLISH INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO ALLEGE A BASIS FOR NEGLIGENCE PER SE AND THE FACTS ALLEGED FAIL TO ESTABLISH A DUTY TO PLAINTIFF. =(302/PJM/PB)
